title: Launch of the NATO Secretary General's Annual Report for 2025
content:
On Thursday, 26 March 2026 , the NATO Secretary General, Mr Mark Rutte, will present his Annual Report for 2025, at NATO Headquarters in Brussels. This will be followed by a short reception for accredited journalists.

Media advisory

+/-14:00 (CET) Press conference by the NATO Secretary General, Press Briefing Room

+/-15:00 (CET) Reception for accredited journalists, NATO Restaurant - Compass room

Media coverage

Media representatives who are annually accredited to NATO in 2026 can cover the press event in person. Annually accredited media who wish to attend the reception are requested to register via this link .

Media representatives without annual accreditation and who are interested in covering the event and the reception are requested to apply for ad-hoc accreditation via this link by 20 March 2026, midnight (CET) . Please apply as early as possible to allow for sufficient time for processing accreditation requests.

The press conference will be streamed live on the NATO website and will be made available to EBU News Exchange and NATO News on YouTube .

A transcript of the Secretary General’s remarks, as well as pictures taken by a NATO photographer, will be available on the NATO website .

Video will be available for free download from the NATO Multimedia Portal after the event.

For more information For general queries: contact the NATO Press Office

Follow us on X: @NATO , @SecGenNATO and @NATOPress

title: NATO Secretary General to take part in the BEDEX 2026 Conference
content:
On Thursday 12 March 2026, the NATO Secretary General, Mr Mark Rutte, will participate in the Brussels European Defence Exhibition & Conference (BEDEX 2026). Mr. Rutte will take part in the official opening of the event and will engage with participants during a panel.

Photographs and videos will be available on the NATO website and video can be downloaded from the NATO Multimedia Portal after the event.

For more information regarding the event, please access the BEDEX website

For general queries: contact the NATO Press Office

Follow us on X: @NATO , @SecGenNATO and @NATOPress

title: NATO Secretary General to meet the Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova
content:
On Wednesday 11 March 2026 , the NATO Secretary General, Mr Mark Rutte, will receive the Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of Moldova, Mr Mihai Popșoi, at NATO Headquarters, in Brussels. Mr. Popșoi will also engage with Allies in the North Atlantic Council.

Media opportunity

09:30 (CET): Remarks by the NATO Secretary General and the Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of Moldova.

Media coverage

Media representatives who are annually accredited to NATO in 2026 can cover the press event in person.

Media representatives without annual accreditation and who are interested in covering the event should email NatoAccreditations@hq.nato.int . Please note that due to the short time to process applications, ad-hoc accreditation may not be guaranteed.

The pool of media covering the remarks will meet in front of the Press Shop at NATO HQ at 09:00 .

The remarks will be streamed live on the NATO website and will be made available to EBU News Exchange and NATO News on YouTube . A transcript of the Secretary General’s remarks, as well as photographs, will be on the NATO website .

Video can be downloaded from the NATO Multimedia Portal after the event.

For more information: For general queries: Contact the NATO Press Office

Follow us on X: @NATO , @SecGenNATO and @NATOPress

content:
On 5 and 6 March 2026 , the NATO Deputy Secretary General, Ms Radmila Shekerinska, will visit Japan.

While in Japan, the NATO Deputy Secretary General will have bilateral meetings with high-level officials. Ms Shekerinska will visit the Japan Air Self-Defense Force, the Atsugi Air Base and the Defence Industry site.

She will also give a keynote speech at the event NATO: The Road Towards Ankara.

There will be no media opportunity.

title: NATO Secretary General to visit the Republic of North Macedonia
content:
On Tuesday 3 March 2026 , the NATO Secretary General, Mr Mark Rutte, will travel to Skopje, North Macedonia, where he will meet with the President of North Macedonia, Ms Gordana Siljanovska Davkova, the Prime Minister, Mr Hristijan Mickoski, and the President of the Assembly, Mr Afrim Gashi. During his visit, the Secretary General will have a joint press conference with the President, and will address the Assembly of the Republic of North Macedonia. Mr. Rutte will also visit the Ilinden Barracks.

Media opportunity

13:00 (CET): Joint press conference by the NATO Secretary General with the President of North Macedonia

13:40 (CET) - Address by the NATO Secretary General to the members of the Assembly

Media coverage

The joint press conference will be streamed live on the NATO website and will be made available to EBU News Exchange and NATO News on YouTube .

Video recording of the joint press conference and the address to the Assembly will be available for free download from the NATO Multimedia Portal after the events.

A transcript of the Secretary General’s remarks during the press conference and address to the Assembly, as well as pictures, will be available on the NATO website .

For more information For general queries: contact the NATO Press Office For accreditation on the ground and other practical details, please contact North Macedonia authorities.

Follow us on X: @NATO , @SecGenNATO and @NATOPress

title: NATO Secretary General to attend the Munich Security Conference
content:
On 13 and 14 February 2026, the NATO Secretary General, Mr Mark Rutte, will attend the Munich Security Conference (MSC), together with the Deputy Secretary General, Ms Radmila Shekerinska.

On 14 February, the Secretary General will participate in a high-level panel discussion at the MSC.

While in Munich, Mr Rutte will also have bilateral meetings with other world leaders and members of the US Congress. He will also participate in a range of events, including the 20 th CSU Transatlantic Forum.

Media advisory

13 February, 10:40 (CET) the Secretary General will have joint press statements with the President of the European Commission, Ms Ursula von der Leyen, Minister for Foreign Affairs, Mr Johann Wadephul, and the Minister-President of Bavaria, Mr Markus Söder.

13 February 13:05 (CET) Doorstep by the NATO Secretary General

14 February, 11:45 (CET) MSC panel discussion with the NATO Secretary General: “Coalition of the Able? Securing Long-Term Support for Ukraine.”

Media coverage

Events described above will be streamed live on the NATO website .

Transcripts of the Secretary General's remarks, as well as pictures, will be available on the NATO website.

For more information: For general queries contact the NATO Press Office For more information regarding the event, please access the MSC Website . Follow us on X: @NATO , @SecGen , @DepSecGen and @NATOPress

title: Meeting of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, with British Counterpart, Yvette Cooper
content:
During her visit to London on 9–10 February 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, held a meeting with the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, Yvette Cooper.

The discussions highlighted the excellent level of bilateral dialogue, based on dynamic political, economic, sectoral and security relations, as well as on a solid Strategic Partnership.

The most recent milestones in the bilateral relationship were recalled, including the visit of the President of Romania, Nicușor Dan, to London in December 2025, which included a private audience with His Majesty King Charles III, as well as the holding, in October 2025 in Bucharest, of the first Romanian–British Strategic Dialogue since the signing of the renewed Joint Declaration on the Strategic Partnership between Romania and the United Kingdom (London, 2023).

The two ministers reconfirmed their shared vision on peace and security, as well as their firm commitment to Euro-Atlantic security, reflected also in the United Kingdom’s participation, within NATO, in missions deployed on Romanian territory.

At the same time, interest was expressed in continuing to capitalize on the outstanding contribution made to strengthening bilateral relations by the more than 1.3 million Romanian citizens living in the United Kingdom.

The two officials noted the dynamic trends in bilateral economic exchanges, highlighting the significant potential for cooperation in the economic field and in terms of mutual investments.

At the proposal of Minister Oana Țoiu, the establishment of a working group on trade and investment was agreed, within the framework of the bilateral strategic partnership. This working group will define priority areas and opportunities of common interest, as well as outline an action plan.

According to available data, in the first ten months of 2025, trade in goods amounted to EUR 3.48 billion, with the United Kingdom ranking 11th among foreign direct investors. The initiative of the Working Group is part of the efforts aimed at stimulating strategic investments in areas such as renewable energy, retail, healthcare, technology and IT.

Boosting Romania’s economic cooperation with the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, one of Romania’s most valuable strategic partners, represented a priority for the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, during the London meeting.

An important positive development welcomed during the discussion with the British counterpart was the entry into force of the Convention between Romania and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland for the elimination of double taxation with respect to taxes on income and capital gains. The conclusion of this Convention reflects the desire of both parties to update the legal framework in the field, previously governed by the bilateral Convention on this matter from 1975.

In order to ensure the rapid and transparent application of the new rules, Minister Oana Țoiu signed, on 29 January, the ministerial order for publication in the Official Gazette, thus completing the administrative steps necessary for the benefits of the Convention to be immediately accessible to businesses and citizens.

The new Convention, which has taken effect in Romania as of January 2026, offers numerous advantages. Alignment with OECD standards provides British and Romanian investors with enhanced fiscal stability and predictability, transparent rules and anti-abuse protection—essential conditions for attracting investment and developing economic cooperation. At the same time, it will serve as the basis for cooperation between the competent tax authorities, with a view to eliminating double taxation, exchanging information, providing assistance in tax collection, and combating unfair tax practices in relations between the two states.

“We share the vision of a more competitive Europe, and for us this objective primarily means stronger partnerships with the United Kingdom. Our economic partnership is robust, with trade exchanges exceeding EUR 10 billion and a positive balance for Romania, while our cooperation as allies keeps our skies and seas safer. Today, we agreed to launch, by this summer, the joint Romania–United Kingdom working group on trade and investment, as well as the roadmap for Romania’s accession to the OECD in 2026. Two excellent pieces of news confirm our concrete actions for business-friendly reforms: today we published in the Official Gazette the final step to avoid double taxation between companies in Romania and the United Kingdom—the MAE ministerial order—and, also today, the fiscal policy milestone required for OECD accession was approved. We also discussed our communities, citizen safety, the fight against organized crime, and the cultural richness they bring to both societies. We additionally addressed the issue of English language courses in Romania, the Romanian language lectureship at Oxford, and expanding access to Romanian language courses in British schools,” Oana Țoiu stated.

During the dialogue, Minister Oana Țoiu highlighted Romania’s substantial progress in the OECD accession process, stressing that the country has advanced at an accelerated pace in fulfilling the roadmap, with a focus on the seven critical milestones planned for the first half of 2026. These milestones directly target the business environment, addressing the corporate governance of state-owned enterprises, the digitalization of tax administration, and alignment with international integrity standards in commercial transactions.

The Romanian official also underlined Romania’s support for strengthening cooperation between the European Union and the United Kingdom, based on their shared commitment to international values, principles and rules.

In the context of discussions on European and Euro-Atlantic security, Minister Oana Țoiu emphasized that Romania will prioritize capability development, improved interoperability, the stimulation of defense production, and the harnessing of innovation, while ensuring complementarity between EU and NATO processes. The Romanian side stressed the importance of continuing the excellent cooperation with British partners, both in combating disinformation and in strengthening strategic communication capabilities.

title: Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu positions Romania as a core transatlantic partner in economic security at the Washington rare minerals ministerial hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and US Vice-president J.D Vance
content:
As a strategic partner of the United States and a core EU member country in the rare minerals domain, Romania joined the first rare minerals ministerial at the invitation of Secretary of state Marco Rubio.

The Washington ministerial and the conversations leading up to it had a clear goal to advance economic security, resilient critical mineral supply chains and global partnerships that protect our industries and jobs in the future.

At a time when Romania’s defence, tech and energy industries have a growing need of resources from global supply chains, being part in international negotiations becomes essential.

Romania is aligned with the United States risk assessment. As part of our Strategic Partnership we fully support the idea to create a global coalition to protect industries from political coercion and supply chain disruption by strengthening transatlantic resilient supply chains through market and policy instruments.

As part of the core group of five European ministers of Foreign Affairs at the ministerial, Romanian minister Oana Țoiu highlighted the fact that we can no longer fully separate economic plans from security needs in this geopolitical context. Minister Țoiu advocated for stronger EU-US ties on investments, processing and common market instruments.

“We all had a wake-up call regarding our supply chain dependencies, but it is the US invitation that accelerated the action to de-risk. Romania is a strong supporter of transatlantic ties. Advancing multilateral agreement on rare minerals is the right path. We will use our strong partnerships to further build prosperity and jobs for our citizens. Predictability for our industries and safe sustainable practices become possible only if those that have price dumping practices face a coordinated response.

We have more than 120 years of working with the US to invest together to bring natural resources into our economies, starting with energy and building further opportunities both bilaterally and in the EU - US framework.

Recognized as a country rich in resource, home of some of the largest copper deposits in Europe, with key projects in magnesium, graphite and resources for magnets, we have a national strategy aimed at increasing investments and the processing of raw materials into finite products. Our GDP has already increased 10 times in a generation, but we are aware that any future growth of our key industries (car industry, energy, tech, defence, appliances) depends on Romania being part of resilient supply chains. We are ready to build them fast” minister Țoiu mentioned in her remarks.

“Romania is emerging as a strategic player in Europe’s critical minerals supply chain. Driven by EU-wide efforts to reduce dependence on external sources, the country has taken bold steps to revive key mining operations and expand exploration activities. The Romanian government, in collaboration with the EU, has prioritized strategic investments and licensing mechanisms”, was the evaluation of the International Trade Organization of the United States of America.

In the next 30 days, a final Memorandum of Understanding, with negotiations led by the EU Commission and the State Department, will advance the development of coordinated trade policies and mechanisms, in the context of the European Critical Raw Material ACT.

Romania is ready to contribute for the defence and high-tech industry in terms of magnets, battery and energy storage, aerospace and defence, green energy transition and also sees opportunities for recycling to increase resource efficiency and sustainability and welcome future European stockpiling plans.

Critical minerals and rare earths are essential for our most advanced technologies and will only become more important as AI, robotics, batteries, and autonomous devices transform our economies.

Background information

Romania attended the 2026 Critical Minerals Ministerial in Washington at the personal invitation of Secretary of State Marco Rubio, joined by Vice President JD Vance, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, Energy Secretary Chris Wright, and U.S. Trade Representative Ambassador Jamieson Greer. The ministerial hosted a core group of 5 ministers of foreign affairs from the EU (Romania, Italy, Poland, Netherlands, Lithuania), and ministers and representatives of 54 countries and the European Commission.

title: NATO Secretary General to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos
content:
From 20 to 22 January 2026, the NATO Secretary General, Mr Mark Rutte, will attend the Annual Meeting of the World Economic Forum (WEF) in Davos, Switzerland.

Mr Rutte will participate in a range of events, including the plenary session. During the World Economic Forum, the Secretary General will also take part in the annual Ukrainian Breakfast discussion, hosted by the Yalta European Strategy and Victor Pinchuk Foundation, and moderated by Fareed Zakaria. He will also have bilateral meetings with other international leaders attending the event.

Media Advisory

21 January

10:15 (CET) Plenary Session: “Can Europe defend itself?”. A panel discussion with the NATO Secretary General, the President of Finland, Mr Alexander Stubb, the President of Poland, Mr Karol Nawrocki, the President of European Investment Bank, Ms Nadia Calviño and the CEO of Sanofi, Mr Paul Hudson.

22 January

7:30 (CET) Davos Ukrainian Breakfast organised by Yalta European Strategy and Victor Pinchuk Foundation. A panel discussion with the NATO Secretary General, the President of Finland, Mr Alexander Stubb, the President of Latvia, Mr Edgars Rinkēvičs, the Prime Minister of the Netherlands, Mr Dick Schoof, the Prime Minister of Belgium, Mr Bart De Wever, the Prime Minister of Croatia, Mr Andrej Plenković and other high level officials.

Media Coverage

The events will be streamed live on the WEF website .

Transcripts of the Secretary General’s remarks will be available on the NATO website

title: Meeting of NATO Ministers of Defence
content:
GENERAL

Allied Defence Ministers will meet at NATO Headquarters in Brussels on 12 February 2026. The meeting will be chaired by the NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte.

Media representatives without annual accreditation to NATO can apply for ad-hoc accreditation to the Ministerial meeting. The deadline for accreditation is Sunday 1 February at 23:59 (CET) .

PROGRAMME

A detailed media programme of the meeting of NATO Defence Ministers will be available on the NATO website closer to the event.

For information about individual Ministers’ media programmes, please contact the national delegations. A list of delegation press officers will be available from NATO Press & Media ( press@hq.nato.int ).

MEDIA ACCREDITATION

Media representatives with an annual accreditation to NATO for 2026 do not need to request accreditation for this event.

All other media representatives wishing to cover the meeting need to apply for accreditation here: link to Accreditation Portal

The deadline to register and apply for media accreditation is Sunday 1 February 2026 at 23:59 (CET) .

Please apply as early as possible to allow for sufficient time for processing accreditation requests.

NATO will confirm accreditation by email. Please bring a printout of the confirmation email when collecting your badge. Media passes must be collected in person upon presentation of the confirmation email, as well as the documents used to apply for accreditation at the Main Entrance, NATO Headquarters, Boulevard Leopold III 1110 Brussels. You must bring the same ID document and the same press card or editor’s letter that you used to apply for accreditation online.

Passes must be visible at all times. Please arrive early to clear security checks. Security personnel will examine and may test equipment and personal effects.

MEDIA ACCESS

Accredited media representatives can work in the press area of NATO Headquarters on the day of the Ministerial meeting, and access all public areas. The opening hours of the press area will be communicated in the media programme.

Accredited journalists can attend Ministers’ doorsteps and the press conferences of the NATO Secretary General. These events will also be streamed live on the NATO website .

National briefings will also take place at NATO Headquarters. Those are by invitation only. Please contact national delegations at NATO for details about their plans.

POOLS

A few elements of the Meeting of NATO Defence Ministers will be accessible only to a pool of visual journalists. Details on pooled events and distribution of pool cards will be available in the media programme, a few days before the event.

If you accept a NATO pool position, you must share immediately all information and material collected while in the pool with any accredited media that requests it, at no charge and with no restriction on the use of the material for news purposes.

Media organisations that want pooled images should first contact the wire service / photo agency of which they are a client. Media representatives and news organisations must identify that it is pooled material every time it is used. Pooled material can only be used for legitimate news purposes and cannot Media be sold.

BROADCAST

NATO will provide broadcast-quality video in real time on EBU World feed or to EBU News Exchange.

NATO photographers and videographers will provide video clips and photos of all public events during the ministerial meeting. Photos can be downloaded from the NATO website . The video files will be available for free download from the NATO Multimedia Portal .

The Secretary General's press conference and public remarks will be streamed live on the NATO website and on NATO YouTube News .

MEDIA FACILITIES

A press working area will be set up on the first floor of the Public Square at NATO HQ. Working spaces cannot be booked in advance and are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Television and radio editing spaces will be available upon request by email to the NATO TV & Radio Unit (see contact details below).

PARKING

Media representatives can park in the Visitors' car park of the NATO HQ. Uplink vans can park in a special section of the car park upon request to the NATO TV & Radio Unit.

For more information:

For general queries: Contact the NATO Press Office

Accreditation NatoAccreditations@hq.nato.int

TV & Radio arrangements on site and via satellite broadcastoperations@hq.nato.int

NATO Video on demand content@natomultimedia.tv

title: Press Release of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs regarding developments in Venezuela
content:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania is closely monitoring the situation in Venezuela, in close coordination with its partners in the European Union.

A community with Romanian roots lives in this country. The first priority for the Romanian Ministry of Foreign Affairs is the safety of Romanian citizens. Romania’s diplomatic and consular missions in Latin America, as well as the Consular Department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, are ready to provide assistance if needed, with the support of the EU Delegation in Caracas. A consular coordination mechanism is in place for EU citizens in Venezuela.

Peace in Venezuela is of the highest concern. The Romanian Ministry of Foreign Affairs urges restraint and responsibility on the part of the authorities.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs also urges the initiation of rapid steps for return to democracy, a goal consistently expressed by Venezuelan citizens.

In the spirit of the statement made by the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs, Kaja Kallas, EU member states are discussing a joint declaration before the convening of a United Nations Security Council meeting on the situation in Venezuela.

We recall that the EU has instituted, with Romania’s agreement, successive sanctions regimes against the undemocratic regime in Caracas, including following the deeply flawed presidential elections in July 2024. The European Union and Romania have not recognized Nicolás Maduro as the legitimate president of Venezuela.

Romania fully shares the priority of combating drug trafficking and organized crime, which represent a significant threat to the security of both continents.

organizations: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania

title: Romania becomes OECD Development Assistance Committee’s (DAC) first associate member
content:
Romania has become the first country to obtain the status of associate member of the Development Assistance Committee (DAC) of the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD), the international forum responsible for coordinating official development assistance provided to developing countries.

Romania's accession to the DAC comes in the context of a steady increase in recent years in the volume of official development assistance provided by our country, as well as the strengthening of Romania's profile as a consistent, predictable, and credible donor at the international level. In 2024, the total value of official development assistance provided by Romania reached USD 539.1 million, representing 0.16% of Gross National Income, the highest level recorded by our country so far. This increase was primarily driven by higher contributions at the multilateral level, especially through allocations to the European Union's development budget.

Romania's policy in the field of international development cooperation is based on the development needs of partner countries and on the national expertise and available capacities, with a focus on areas such as institutional development, the green transition, environmental protection, and combating climate change, as well as human development. Official development assistance activities are directed toward ten priority countries in the extended Black Sea region, the Western Balkans, the Middle East, and Africa. The Republic of Moldova represents Romania's top partner, with our country actively supporting the European path of the neighboring state, including within the framework of the Strategic Partnership between Romania and the Republic of Moldova for the European integration of the Republic of Moldova .

Being an Associate Member of the OECD Development Assistance Committee provides Romania with an opportunity to make better use of its comparative advantages and donor experience, while contributing to strengthening and making more effective the national system for delivering official development assistance and increasing the impact of international development cooperation actions for the benefit of partner countries.

The OECD Development Assistance Committee was established in 1961 and originated from the Development Aid Group set up in 1960 within the framework of the Organisation for European Economic Cooperation (OEEC). With the transformation of the OEEC into the OECD, the DAC became a permanent body of the Organization. The Committee brings together the main global providers of official development assistance, aiming to promote cooperation for development and policies that contribute to sustainable development, poverty alleviation, reducing inequalities and improving living standards in developing countries.

Main steps in the process of Romania’s accession to the OECD Development Assistance Committee:

• On July 4, 2024, at the proposal of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Romanian Government approved the initiation of the process of Romania's accession to the OECD Development Assistance Committee.

• On July 5, 2024, the Minister of Foreign Affairs sent a formal letter to the President of the Committee expressing Romania's interest in joining as an associate member.

• On December 19, 2024, Romania sent information to the OECD regarding its compliance with the criteria and conditions for joining the Development Assistance Committee.

• From July 7–10, 2025, the OECD carried out an evaluation mission in Bucharest to assess Romania's compliance with the criteria and conditions for accession to the Development Assistance Committee.

• On November 7, 2025, the OECD Development Assistance Committee decided that Romania met the criteria and conditions for joining the Committee.

• On December 16, 2025, the OECD Council approved the invitation for Romania to join the OECD Development Assistance Committee and sent an invitation letter to Romania's Minister of Foreign Affairs from the OECD Secretary-General.

• On December 17, 2025, Romania obtained the status of associate member of the OECD Development Assistance Committee, following the acceptance of the invitation by Romania's Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, through a letter sent to OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann.

The OECD DAC press release is available at: Romania joins the OECD Development Assistance Committee as its first Associate

title: Romania launches, in Addis Ababa, a training programme on post-conflict reconstruction and combating disinformation in Africa
content:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs, through the Romanian Agency for International Development (RoAid), in partnership with the Institute of Foreign Affairs (IFA) of Ethiopia, launched on Monday, October 20, in Addis Ababa, the training programme “Post-conflict Stabilization and Reconstruction – Best Practices in Building Resilience and Fighting Disinformation” .

The programme, taking place from 20–24 October 2025, brings together experts, diplomats, researchers, and representatives of public institutions and civil society from Ethiopia, other African countries, and Romania, with the aim of sharing best practices and experience in post-conflict reconstruction, crisis management, and combating disinformation.

The opening ceremony was addressed by H.E. Jafar Bedru Geletu, Executive Director of the Institute of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia, H.E. Iulia Pataki, Ambassador of Romania to the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia, and Ms. Daniela Dobre, Director General of the Romanian Agency for International Development – RoAid.

In her remarks, Ambassador Iulia Pataki highlighted that this programme represents a concrete Romanian initiative supporting peace and stability in Africa through expertise sharing, professional training, and inter-institutional dialogue. “Romania is firmly committed to contributing to international post-conflict reconstruction efforts and to countering threats generated by disinformation and manipulation, which affect democracies across all regions of the world,” the Ambassador stated.

Throughout the week, participants will engage in lectures and interactive exercises led by Romanian and Ethiopian experts, together with specialists from the Institute of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia.

The sessions will cover topics such as institutional resilience, combating disinformation, the role of civil society and women in stabilization processes, and cooperation between the European Union, African Union, and international partners in the field of peace and security.

Through this initiative, Romania reaffirms its commitment to supporting sustainable development, peace, and stability in Africa, in line with the EU–Africa Partnership and Romania’s International Development Cooperation Strategy.

Additional information:

The programme is implemented by the Romanian Agency for International Development – RoAid, under the coordination of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, as part of Romania’s development cooperation initiatives. RoAid supports partner countries in strengthening institutional capacities, managing crisis situations, and achieving sustainable development goals.

title: Press release  Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu in the Inauguration Ceremony for the Works Related to the Modernization Project of Unit 1 of the Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant
content:
On Wednesday, September 3, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu, together with President Nicușor Dan, attended the inauguration ceremony for the civil construction works within the Project for the Modernization of Unit 1 of the Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant.

On this occasion, the Minister of Foreign Affairs commended the progress made on a strategic project that will contribute to Romania's energy security, job creation and sustainable economic development. The Minister also highlighted Romania's cooperation with like-minded, reliable partners such as Canada, the US, South Korea and Italy in the field of civil nuclear energy.

Modernizing Unit 1 will guarantee another 30 years of clean, safe and reliable energy, as well as continuing more than four decades of cooperation with Canada in the nuclear field. This collaboration has contributed to establish Romania as a center of expertise in nuclear energy.

Upgrading Unit 1 will strengthen Romania's energy production capacity, supporting the provision of reliable, competitively priced energy to domestic consumers. At the same time, it will help our country to achieve its ambition of becoming a regional hub capable of contributing to the energy security of neighboring countries and the wider region.

Additional information

Unit 1 of the Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant began commercial operation in 1996, with an installed capacity of 700 MWe. The modernization of Unit 1 is a strategic project for Societatea Naţională Nuclearelectrica S.A. (SNN), with the aim of extending the life of the CANDU reactor by 30 years from 2029 onwards.

In December 2024, S.N. Nuclearelectrica, the project leader, awarded an Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) contract for the modernization of Unit 1 at Cernavodă. The consortium of partners consists of Candu Energy Inc., the Canadian Commercial Corporation, Ansaldo Nucleare S.p.A. and Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power Co. Ltd.

The Canadian, South Korean, and Italian ambassadors also attended the inauguration ceremony.

Romania plays an active and consistent role in promoting the use of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes. It is a firm supporter of using nuclear energy to produce electricity safely and sustainably, in accordance with international non-proliferation and nuclear safety standards.

title: Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, Received Montenegro's Ambassador to Romania, Danilo Brajović, on the Occasion of the Presentation of His Credentials
content:
On Tuesday, September 2, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu received Montenegro's Ambassador to Romania, Danilo Brajović, for the presentation of copies of his letters of credence.

The Romanian minister commended the dynamic progress of the highest-level bilateral political dialogue, as well as that between the foreign ministries of the two countries. She encouraged maintaining this pace, including as a means of boosting sectoral cooperation and diversifying economic exchanges. The two officials emphasized the essential complementarity between economic diplomacy and political-diplomatic dialogue, and expressed their willingness to support the finalization and signing of bilateral sectoral cooperation instruments.

The Romanian official reaffirmed the full support for Montenegro's European path, commending the ambitious goals of the Government in Podgorica and emphasizing the importance of maintaining the pace and quality of the reforms undertaken. In line with the commitments made, Romania remains open to continuing to offer expertise in support of Montenegro's efforts to strengthen its administrative capacity and implement structural reforms.

The good cooperation within NATO was appreciated, with particular reference to the contributions of the two countries to collective defense and regional security, especially on the Eastern Flank.

At the end of the meeting, Minister Oana Țoiu wished Ambassador Danilo Brajović every success in his work in Romania, assuring him of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' full support in fulfilling his mandate.

title: Romania, a Reliable Partner in a Decade of Changes: Conclusions of the 2025 Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy
content:
Romania's active role in strengthening regional security, accelerating economic diplomacy, and advancing efforts to join the OECD were among the main topics on the agenda of the 2025 edition of the Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy (RADR), which ended on Tuesday, August 26.

The working sessions, which took place at the Palace of Parliament, were attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, and were themed "A decade of changes: the impact of Romanian diplomacy". The debates confirmed Romania's priorities of strengthening regional security, accelerating economic diplomacy and advancing efforts to join the OECD.

RADR 2025 provided an opportunity for dialogue with ministers, Romanian and foreign officials, representatives of European and Euro-Atlantic institutions, as well as with the members of economic and academic communities.

The President of the Senate, Mircea Abrudean; the President of the Chamber of Deputies, Sorin Grindeanu; and the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, who represent Romania's central administration, participated in and spoke at the opening session. Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan also engaged in an open and honest dialogue with Romania's ambassadors and consuls general during a special session.

During the plenary working sessions, the following foreign guests addressed the heads of diplomatic missions and consular offices:

This year's edition also featured a video message from Elina Valtonen, the Minister for Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Finland, and the participation of Luminița Odobescu, the Presidential Advisor, in the working session dedicated to the future of the European Union.

'You can count on Romania! Romania remains a predictable and supportive partner, focused on achieving concrete results for its citizens and the international community,' stated Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu. She also emphasized that Romanian diplomacy had played a decisive role in turning the challenges of the last decade into opportunities for Romania and the region.

The main messages and outcomes of the 2025 edition were as follows:

Romania - a predictable and reliable partner . The central message of RADR 2025 was that Romania is committed to regional stability, European and transatlantic cooperation, and supporting Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova.

Security and resilience in our neighborhood. Discussions during the session dedicated to the 'new strategic reality' emphasized the importance of strengthening deterrence and defense in the Black Sea region, providing comprehensive support to Ukraine and promoting the European integration of the Republic of Moldova .

Regional coordination and partnerships . Bilateral consultations between Romania and the Republic of Moldova focused on interconnections, critical infrastructure projects and sectoral cooperation, including European affairs.

Economic diplomacy for competitiveness . Participants emphasized the importance of diplomatic missions in promoting investment and exports, connecting Romanian companies to value chains and facilitating the adoption of OECD standards by the economy and administration. The goal of joining the OECD by 2026 was reaffirmed as a working benchmark.

Additional information:

The Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy is the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' main forum for strategic reflection, through which the lines of action for the diplomatic network and Romania's foreign policy priorities are established.

RADR 2025 took place on 25–26 August and featured thematic sessions dedicated to economic diplomacy, the OECD, the new regional strategic reality, the future of the European Union and the prospect of an enlarged, safer and more prosperous Europe. The sessions were moderated by Secretaries of State from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs: Clara Staicu, Ana Tinca and Luca Niculescu.

On the margins of the meeting, bilateral consultations were held between Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and her Moldovan counterpart Mihai Popșoi, followed by a joint press conference. ( https://www.mae.ro/node/67258 ).

title: Consultations between Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu and Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova, Mihai Popșoi
content:
On Tuesday, August 26, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu held consultations with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova Mihai Popșoi, who was in Bucharest to participate in the Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy.

Following the meeting, the two officials held a press conference, available here: https://www.mae.ro/node/67256 . The transcript of the statement by the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs is available here: https://www.mae.ro/node/67257 .

Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu extended her congratulations to her counterpart and the citizens of the Republic of Moldova on the occasion of their Independence Day, which is celebrated annually on August 27. She also expressed her gratitude to Deputy Prime Minister Popșoi for taking part in this year's RADR event, which was held under the title "A decade of change: the impact of Romanian diplomacy."

The agenda included topical issues related to the Republic of Moldova's accession process to the European Union, as well as developments in bilateral projects in important areas of cooperation, such as: energy, infrastructure, border crossing points, education and research. The meeting also addressed ways to strengthen the stability and resilience of the Republic of Moldova.

In light of the significant developments taking place at the regional and international levels that are also important for the European future of the Republic of Moldova, Minister Oana Țoiu commended the efforts made by the authorities in Chișinău to promote the EU accession process. The head of Romanian diplomacy reiterated the firm commitment of the President and Government of Romania to supporting the European integration of the Republic of Moldova. This message was also conveyed by Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan a few days ago during his visit to Chisinau on August 23, 2025.

The two officials discussed the current status and prospects for further progress in the following areas: energy interconnections (through high-voltage lines); land transport infrastructure (including the construction of bridges, the rehabilitation of the Iași-Ungheni railway connection, and its electrification); the strengthening of the Republic of Moldova's energy independence (including by supporting its domestic electricity generation capacity); and the streamlining of traffic through crossing points of common border

At the same time, Minister Oana Țoiu commended the Republic of Moldova's authorities for their efforts to prevent and combat foreign interference attempts in the lead-up to the September 28, 2025 parliamentary elections. In this context, she stressed that Romania will continue to strongly support the Republic of Moldova in strengthening its resilience, which is essential to ensuring the integrity of the upcoming electoral process.

title: Minister Oana Țoiu Engaged in an Open Dialogue with Representatives of the Concordia Employers' Confederation to Discuss Ways of Promoting Romania's Economy
content:
On Tuesday, August 12,2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, met with representatives of the Concordia Employers' Confederation to discuss Romania's priorities in the field of economic diplomacy.

The minister emphasized that, especially now, dialogue with the business community is not only useful, but absolutely necessary. She expressed her appreciation for Concordia's active involvement both domestically and internationally, including its participation in platforms such as Business at OECD (BIAC), which promotes the interests of Romanian entrepreneurs.

Minister Oana Țoiu invited the private sector to propose ways in which economic diplomacy instruments, such as bilateral missions and contacts, thematic events, analyses and specialized materials, could support Romanian companies. She commended the Concordia representatives' proposals, particularly those regarding the reform of the economic diplomacy instruments available to the Romanian state, including the Romanian Agency for Investment and Foreign Trade (ARICE).

Oana Țoiu also mentioned her recent meetings with high-ranking Ukrainian officials she had during her bilateral visit from 7 to 9 August and called on Romanian companies to become involved in the reconstruction process of the neighboring country. She encouraged them to participate in tenders and investments in Ukraine, emphasising the significant opportunities for the development of Romanian businesses.
